function.name: omicsclaw
function.description:
Run an OmicsClaw multi-omics analysis skill.

(test)

PREFER `skill='auto'` + `query=<user request>` (auto-routes deterministically). Pick a specific skill only if the user named it. `mode='demo'` ONLY when explicitly asked. For a server-side file, use `mode='path'` and pass the path at the top level as `file_path='/abs/path.h5ad'` — do NOT nest args under a `params` key. `mode='file'` is only for files the user uploaded via chat. `return_media` empty = text summary; pass a keyword ('umap','qc','cluster',comma-sep) or 'all' for figures. For sc-batch-integration upstream-prep pause, rerun with `auto_prepare=true`. Preserve exact numbers, warnings, errors, and file paths when relaying results.

skill.description: Skill alias to run. Default to 'auto' and pass `query`; use a specific alias only if the user named it or auto-routing asked you to disambiguate.

mode.description: 'demo' = built-in synthetic data; 'file' = user uploaded a file via messaging; 'path' = user provided a file path on the server.

return_media.description: Filter for which figures/tables to send back. Omit or leave empty for text summary only (default). 'all' = send all figures and tables. Otherwise a comma-separated list of keywords to match filenames (e.g. 'umap', 'qc', 'violin', 'cluster', 'umap,qc'). Only set when the user explicitly asks for visual results.

file_path.description: Server-side file path or filename for mode='path'.

batch_key.description: Batch/sample metadata column passed as --batch-key. Especially important for sc-batch-integration. If omitted for sc-batch-integration, OmicsClaw will inspect the AnnData object and ask the user to choose before running.

confirm_workflow_skip.description: Only for explicit user overrides. If true, bypasses the standardize/preprocess workflow pause for sc-batch-integration and runs direct integration anyway.

auto_prepare.description: Only when the user explicitly agrees to preparatory steps. For sc-batch-integration, automatically runs the recommended upstream workflow (`sc-standardize-input` and/or `sc-preprocessing`) before the final integration step.

n_epochs.description: Number of training epochs for deep learning methods. Defaults per method if omitted: cell2location=30000, destvi=2500, stereoscope=150000, tangram=1000. Only set when the user explicitly requests a custom epoch count.

function.name: replot_skill
function.description: Re-render R Enhanced (ggplot2) plots from a prior skill output WITHOUT re-running. Use for 're-draw with R', 'make it prettier', 'replot', or to tune params. Skill must have run first via `omicsclaw`. ALWAYS call for R Enhanced — never assume lack of R support. Use `renderer` to pick one sub-plot; tune via `top-n`/`font-size`/`width`/`height`/`dpi`/`palette`/`title`. Omit `output_path` to auto-resolve. If R missing, relay returned install instructions; do NOT fall back to `custom_analysis_execute` or Python plotting.

output_path.description: Full path to the output directory from the previous skill run. Omit to auto-resolve from session history.

renderer.description: Optional: run only a specific renderer (e.g. 'plot_feature_violin'). Omit to run all renderers.

return_media.description: Filter for which R Enhanced figures to send. Default: 'all'. Use a keyword to filter (e.g. 'violin').

top_n.description: Number of top items to label/show.

function.name: list_skills_in_domain
function.description: Lazy-load the full skill list for one OmicsClaw domain. Call this ONLY when the 8-domain briefing in the `omicsclaw` tool description isn't enough to pick a skill, or when the user asks 'what tools do you have for <domain>?'. The result is a markdown block with each skill's alias, one-line description, and trigger keywords. For most routing, prefer `omicsclaw(skill='auto', query=...)` — the resolver picks the right skill without this extra round-trip.

filter.description: Optional case-insensitive substring. Matches against skill alias, description, and trigger keywords. Omit to see the whole domain.

function.name: save_file
function.description: Save a file that was sent via messaging to a specific folder. Default: OmicsClaw data/ directory.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Files are saved to the output/ directory by default. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to create/save a file.

function.name: generate_audio
function.description: Generate an MP3 audio file from text using edge-tts.

function.name: parse_literature
function.description: Parse scientific literature (PDF, URL, DOI, PubMed ID) to extract GEO accessions and metadata, then download datasets. Use when user mentions a paper, sends a PDF, or provides a literature reference.

input_value.description: URL, DOI, PubMed ID, file path, or text content

function.name: fetch_geo_metadata
function.description: Fetch metadata for a specific GEO accession (GSE, GSM, or GPL). Use when user asks to fetch, query, or get information about a specific GEO ID.

download.description: Download the dataset after fetching metadata (default: false)

function.name: list_directory
function.description: List contents of a directory. Use when user wants to see files in a folder.

function.name: inspect_file
function.description: Display contents of a CSV, JSON, or TXT file. Use when user wants to view file contents.

function.name: make_directory
function.description: Create a new directory under output/. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to create a folder.

function.name: move_file
function.description: Move or rename a file. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to move or rename files.

function.name: remove_file
function.description: Delete a file or directory. ONLY use when user explicitly asks to remove files/folders.

path.description: File or directory path to remove

function.name: get_file_size
function.description: Get file size in MB. Use when user asks about file size.

function.name: remember
function.description: Save important information to persistent memory so you can recall it in future conversations. Call this (not `task_create`) for '记住 X' / 'remember X' requests. Use to remember: user preferences (language, default methods, DPI settings), biological insights (cell type annotations, spatial domains found), and project context (research goals, species, tissue type, disease model). Memory persists across conversations and bot restarts.

memory_type.description: Type of memory to save. 'preference' = user settings (language, default method, DPI). 'insight' = biological discovery (cell types, clusters). 'project_context' = research context (species, tissue, disease, goal).

key.description: For preference: setting name (e.g. 'language', 'default_method', 'dpi'). For insight: entity ID (e.g. 'cluster_0', 'domain_3'). For project_context: not used.

value.description: For preference: setting value (e.g. 'Chinese', 'tangram', '300'). For insight: biological label (e.g. 'T cells', 'tumor region'). For project_context: not used.

domain.description: For preference: scope of the setting (e.g. 'global', 'spatial-preprocess'). Default: 'global'.

source_analysis_id.description: For insight: ID of the analysis that produced this insight (optional).

confidence.description: For insight: confidence level. Use 'user_confirmed' when user explicitly states a label.

function.name: recall
function.description: Retrieve information from persistent memory. Use this to recall: user preferences, biological insights, project context, dataset info, and analysis history saved in previous conversations. You can search by keyword or list memories by type.

query.description: Search query to find specific memories. E.g. 'language preference', 'cluster annotations', 'species'.

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return (default: 10).

function.name: forget
function.description: Remove a specific memory from persistent storage. Use when user explicitly asks to forget or remove a saved preference, insight, or other memory. Provide a query or memory_id to identify which memory to remove.

memory_id.description: The memory_id of the memory to forget (if known).

query.description: Search query to find the memory to forget.

function.name: read_knowhow
function.description: Fetch the full markdown body of a Mandatory Scientific Constraint (KnowHow guard) by name. Call this when the headline-only Active Guards block in the system prompt does not give enough detail — for example, when the user asks WHY a guard requires a specific threshold, when a method name is ambiguous, or when the headline mentions parameters the user is uncertain about. Accepts the filename (e.g. KH-sc-de-guardrails.md), the doc_id (sc-de-guardrails), or the human-readable label. Returns the full markdown text, or an empty string if no guard matches.

name.description: KH identifier. Accepts filename (KH-sc-de-guardrails.md), doc_id (sc-de-guardrails), or label (Single-Cell Differential Expression Guardrails).

function.name: consult_knowledge
function.description: Query the OmicsClaw knowledge base for analysis guidance. Use this PROACTIVELY when: (1) user is unsure which analysis to run, (2) user asks about method selection or parameters, (3) an analysis fails and user needs troubleshooting, (4) user asks 'how to' or 'which method' questions, (5) before running complex analyses to check best practices. Returns relevant decision guides, best practices, or troubleshooting advice.

query.description: Natural language question about methodology, parameters, or troubleshooting

function.name: resolve_capability
function.description: Resolve whether a user request is fully covered by an existing OmicsClaw skill, partially covered, or not covered. Use this before non-trivial analysis requests when skill coverage is uncertain.

query.description: The user's analysis request in natural language.

file_path.description: Optional server-side data path or filename mentioned by the user.

domain_hint.description: Optional domain hint if the user has already narrowed the domain.

function.name: web_method_search
function.description: Search external web sources for up-to-date method documentation, papers, or workflow guidance. Use this when resolve_capability indicates no_skill or partial_skill and external references are needed.

query.description: Search query describing the method, package, or analysis goal.

max_results.description: Maximum number of search results to fetch. Default: 3.

function.name: create_omics_skill
function.description: Create a new OmicsClaw-native skill scaffold under skills/<domain>/<skill-name>/ using the canonical v2 layout (SKILL.md + parameters.yaml sidecar + references/{methodology,output_contract,parameters,r_visualization}.md). The emitted skill is lint-clean against scripts/skill_lint.py and ready to register via the runtime registry. Use this only when the user explicitly wants a reusable new skill added to OmicsClaw. If a previous custom_analysis_execute run succeeded, you can promote that notebook into the new skill.

request.description: Original user request describing the desired reusable skill.

domain.description: Target OmicsClaw domain for the new skill. Optional if it can be inferred from a promoted autonomous analysis.

summary.description: One-line summary of what the skill should do.

source_analysis_dir.description: Optional path to a successful custom_analysis_execute output directory to promote into a skill.

promote_from_latest.description: If true, promote the most recent successful autonomous analysis output when source_analysis_dir is not provided.

input_formats.description: Optional list of domain-specific input format notes.

primary_outputs.description: Optional list of primary outputs the new skill should emit.

methods.description: Optional list of primary methods/backends for the skill.

trigger_keywords.description: Optional list of routing keywords users may say naturally.

create_tests.description: Whether to generate a minimal test scaffold. Default: true.

function.name: autonomous_analysis_execute
function.description: Recommended generated-code path for partial/no-skill analysis. Provide the goal and optional local/web context; OmicsClaw writes, executes, and repairs bounded Python/R code in an isolated autonomous workspace.

goal.description: Short statement of the analysis objective.

context.description: Optional local context from the conversation or prior skill outputs.

web_context.description: Optional external method notes already retrieved by web_method_search.

input_paths.description: Optional original data paths. They are referenced, not copied.

upstream_paths.description: Optional prior skill output directories to consume as references.

max_repair_attempts.description: Maximum evidence-bound repairs after the first execution. Default: 2, max: 2.

data_schema.description: Inspected input data schema (obs/var/obsm/layers/uns keys, shape, platform) already provided to you in context. Pass it through so generated code and repairs use real keys instead of guessing.

analysis_plan.description: Your concrete, data-grounded plan formed before delegating: steps, chosen method, expected outputs, and any stated assumptions.

function.name: custom_analysis_execute
function.description: Legacy adapter for user-supplied Python snippets in a restricted notebook. Prefer autonomous_analysis_execute for new generated-code analysis because it owns code generation, execution, repair, and autonomous workspace reports.

goal.description: Short statement of the analysis objective.

analysis_plan.description: Concise markdown plan describing what the custom analysis will do.

python_code.description: A single self-contained Python snippet to execute inside the restricted notebook.

context.description: Optional local context from the conversation or prior skill outputs.

web_context.description: Optional external method notes returned by web_method_search.

file_path.description: Optional input file path to expose as INPUT_FILE inside the notebook.

sources.description: Optional markdown list of cited URLs or source notes to persist alongside the notebook.

output_label.description: Optional short label for the output directory prefix.

function.name: inspect_data
function.description: Instantly inspect an AnnData (.h5ad) file's metadata — cell/gene counts, obs/var columns, embeddings (obsm), layers, uns keys — WITHOUT loading the expression matrix. Fast even for very large files. Also supports pre-run method suitability and parameter preview when `method` (and optionally `skill`) is provided. ALWAYS call this when the user asks to 'explore', 'inspect', 'what can I do with', or vaguely 'analyze' a dataset WITHOUT specifying a concrete analysis pipeline. Returns a formatted summary and suggested analysis directions. Do NOT call omicsclaw for open-ended exploratory queries.

file_path.description: Path to the .h5ad file to inspect.

skill.description: Optional skill alias for preflight preview (e.g. spatial-domain-identification).

method.description: Optional method name for suitability + parameter preview.

preview_params.description: If true, include a pre-run method suitability and default parameter preview block.

function.name: tool_search
function.description: Search available OmicsClaw tools by name, description, and tags. Use this before assuming a tool exists.

query.description: Search text for tool names, capabilities, or tags.

limit.description: Maximum number of tools to return. Default: 12.

include_schema.description: Include parameter names in the response.

function.name: file_read
function.description: Read a text file from the active workspace, pipeline workspace, or trusted project directories.

end_line.description: 1-based ending line number. Default: end of file.

function.name: glob_files
function.description: Find files under the active workspace or project directories using a glob pattern.

root.description: Optional root directory to search from.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 200.

function.name: grep_files
function.description: Search text files under the active workspace or project directories using a regular expression.

root.description: Optional root directory to search from.

glob.description: Optional glob filter for candidate files. Default: '**/*'.

max_matches.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Default: 50.

function.name: file_write
function.description: Create or overwrite a text file inside the active workspace or a safe engineering output workspace.

create_dirs.description: Create parent directories when missing. Default: true.

function.name: file_edit
function.description: Perform a controlled text replacement inside an existing text file.

replace_all.description: Replace all matches instead of exactly one match.

function.name: task_create
function.description: Create a persisted engineering task for the current session. Not for '记住 X' / 'remember X' — use `remember` for those.

function.name: task_get
function.description: Read one persisted engineering task for the current session.

function.name: task_list
function.description: List persisted engineering tasks for the current session.

function.name: task_update
function.description: Update status or details for a persisted engineering task. Use this to keep the live 待办 plan accurate: set status 'in_progress' right before working a step and 'completed' (or 'failed'/'skipped') right after, keeping exactly one step 'in_progress' at a time.

summary.description: Short execution summary stored in metadata.summary.

artifact_ref.description: Artifact path or identifier to append.

function.name: todo_write
function.description: Replace the current session task list with a structured todo plan. Use this at the start of a multi-step analysis to lay out 3–7 concrete ordered steps (each status 'pending'); the desktop shows this plan live to the user. After writing it, advance progress with `task_update` (in_progress → completed) rather than rewriting the whole list each step.

function.name: web_fetch
function.description: Fetch a webpage and convert the result to markdown for local reasoning.

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for method documentation, package docs, workflow guidance, or external references.

query.description: Search query describing the method or topic.

max_results.description: Maximum number of search results to fetch. Default: 3.

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Ask the user to choose among a small set of options when their intent is ambiguous or a decision is needed before proceeding. The desktop shows the question as an interactive choice card. After calling this, STOP and wait for the user's reply — do not call other tools or answer on their behalf.

question.description: The question to ask the user.

header.description: Optional short label/category for the question.

allow_custom.description: Whether the user may type their own answer.
